title: Evangelion 222 will not be eligible for the 83rd Oscars
display_title: Evangelion 222 will not be eligible for the 83rd Oscars
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 2.22 will not be eligible for the 83rd Oscars.             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Nov 28, 2010 11:10 pm Just in case this hasn't been stated prominently in one of the other topics, I thought I would point it out. I double checked on Oscar eligibility, Evangelion 2.22 will NOT be eligible for this year's Oscars. It needs to be screened for seven consecutive days for paid admission in a commercial motion picture theater in Los Angeles County, prior to midnight on December 31st, 2010. If they don't screen it until January, then it's not going to happen this year. http://www.oscars.org/awards/academyawards/rules/rule02.html Funi has already acknowledged this (I heard it at their panel at Anime USA). They're working on getting Summer Wars on the ballot instead. They're just doing the theatrical run of 2.22 because they want to.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Mon Nov 29, 2010 2:51 am Well,it's not like it really mattered if it was eligable or not. Sure,would of been nice if an anime like this had a chance at something like this but it isnt a big deal that it didnt make the cut.

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Mon Nov 29, 2010 2:55 am I really dont find this outcome being surprising at all. Most audiences and judges voting for the winner in each of these film categories probably wouldnt give a flying fuck that its an Eva movie. On its own Eva 2.0 is not that strong of a film, especially when viewed by critics and judges voting for the Academy. Funimation acted smart this time and submitted a film, namely Summer Wars, thats not only more creative and imaginative, but also has more heart and stronger characters. Well characters that at least thematically seem stronger. We'll see....how this goes.

[]:             Quote Post by KaaVI » Mon Nov 29, 2010 5:49 am I think it's better if 2.22 didn't run for this year's but next years. As if it had a chance against the likes of TOY STORY 3. Just being realistic. If it were to run for next year's oscars then, yes it would have a chance.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Nov 29, 2010 7:59 am Sure it wouldn't have a chance of winning. That's not really the concern. It's about exposure. All of the judges would have to watch it, and it would appear in a variety of Oscars materials.

[]:             Quote Post by Naminator » Mon Nov 29, 2010 8:24 am I personally would have loved if Evangelion would win an Oscar. Or any high quality anime for that matter. Maybe it would inspire the anime industry to pick up their shit and bring us some good anime. Not to mention it would most likely bring more exposure to anime in the west. Ohhh I can dream can't I?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Nov 29, 2010 11:31 am If they'd submitted 2.22 it would have given Summer Wars a better chance because there would be five nominees instead of three.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Nov 29, 2010 2:38 pm We all know that all those awards are biased against any sort of Animation. Who cares if 2.22 outsold Michael Jackson, if it's animated, it's not "serious" in their Eyes. Jerks. With Animation, you can show whatever you want without having to worry about how to film it, but no, it HAS to be cheap kids stuff, even 2.22's almost photorealistic scenery shots.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Nov 29, 2010 3:45 pm Kendrix wrote: We all know that all those awards are biased against any sort of Animation.

[]:             Quote Post by KaaVI » Tue Nov 30, 2010 5:38 pm I think it's better if they nominate it for next year. Ive seen all the animated movies that would be nominated for the 84th, I've had a look at all the american made animations and i don't see any real competition.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Tue Nov 30, 2010 6:25 pm KaaVI wrote: I think it's better if they nominate it for next year. Ive seen all the animated movies that would be nominated for the 84th, I've had a look at all the american made animations and i don't see any real competition.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue Nov 30, 2010 9:25 pm even if it was eligible, I do not think any Rebuild film would get any kind of Oscar nom my irrational hate for the Academy aside, the Academy cannot get away from the Ghibli-type anime, even something like GitS IMO would be far too out there in terms of the Academy's "standards" for animation Pixar animation is as edgy as the Academy gets at this point

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Nov 30, 2010 9:42 pm KaaVI wrote: I think it's better if they nominate it for next year.

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Tue Nov 30, 2010 10:41 pm KaaVI wrote: I think it's better if they nominate it for next year. Ive seen all the animated movies that would be nominated for the 84th, I've had a look at all the american made animations and i don't see any real competition.

[]:             Quote Post by KaaVI » Wed Dec 01, 2010 5:12 am Electric Sachiel wrote: Are you joking? Toy Story 3 and How to train your Dragon are definitely not competitors to take lightly during this coming Oscars.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Dec 01, 2010 9:01 am KaaVI wrote: How to train your dragon was complete utter crap Eva would have easily won over that movie.

[]:             Quote Post by Nonoriri » Wed Dec 01, 2010 12:39 pm KaaVI wrote: Hence why i said it would be better for Eva to be nominated in the 84th. It would get more recognized and not overshadowed by the likes of Toy story 3. How to train your dragon was complete utter crap Eva would have easily won over that movie.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Dec 01, 2010 12:48 pm Nonoriri wrote: There is not a reality in which this were even possible.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Dec 01, 2010 1:09 pm Nonoriri wrote: There is not a reality in which this were even possible.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Wed Dec 01, 2010 1:23 pm KaaVI wrote: How to train your dragon was complete utter crap Eva would have easily won over that movie.
